records.ety: [F. transposer; pref. trans- (L. trans across) + poser to put. See Pose.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Transposed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Transposing.]

defs: To change the place or order of; to substitute one for the other of; to exchange, in respect of position; as, to transpose letters, words, or propositions.
To change; to transform; to invert.
quotes: Things base and vile, holding no quantity, Love can transpose to form and dignity.

defs: To bring, as any term of an equation, from one side over to the other, without destroying the equation; thus, if a + b = c, and we make a = c - b, then b is said to be transposed.
To change the natural order of, as words.
